generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance OccupancyActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building control actuator for occupancy-based lighting and HVAC adjustments in LEED Platinum academic building"
]

instance HVACActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "HVAC control unit for temperature and humidity regulation in the LEED Platinum academic building's living lab environment"
]

instance LightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Smart lighting control system implementing adaptive TwinLight algorithms for visual comfort optimization"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Post-occupancy environmental sensor measuring real-time temperature data in the LEED Platinum academic building's living lab"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance HumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Post-occupancy environmental sensor measuring real-time humidity levels in the LEED Platinum academic building's living lab"
    DTDFVocab:producedData HumidityDataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Post-occupancy occupancy sensor detecting real-time tenant presence and activity patterns in the LEED Platinum academic building's living lab"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyMonitor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Energy consumption monitoring device tracking real-time power usage metrics in the LEED Platinum academic building's living lab"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyDataTransmitted
]

instance CO2Sensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Indoor air quality sensor measuring real-time CO2 concentration levels in the LEED Platinum academic building's living lab"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CO2DataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance HumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HumiditySensor
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance EnergyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyMonitor
]

instance CO2DataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CO2Sensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BIMStaticModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Semantically enriched 3D BIM model of the LEED Platinum academic building's living lab components with embedded BCS metadata"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BIMModelDrivenEngineering
]

instance RuntimeOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured real-time operational data repository for the LEED Platinum academic building's living lab processed via Ditto middleware"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted, HumidityDataTransmitted, OccupancyDataTransmitted, EnergyDataTransmitted, CO2DataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoPlatform
]

instance ControlLogicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Formalized control strategies (e.g., adaptive lighting algorithms) embedded in BIM models for automated runtime interface generation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TwinLightControlFramework
]

instance VisualizationModel : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Unreal Engine 5-based 3D DT visualization for interactive monitoring and simulation of the LEED Platinum academic building's living lab"
    DTDFVocab:fromData RuntimeOperationalData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UnrealEngine5Environment
]

instance BIMMetadataData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Extracted BIM metadata containing sensor/actuator configurations, tenant IDs, and endpoint addresses for DT runtime configuration"
    DTDFVocab:fromData BIMStaticModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BIMModelDrivenEngineering
]

// C11: Enablers
instance BIMModelDrivenEngineering : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Driven Engineering framework automating transformation of BIM models into runtime artifacts via M2M/M2T transformations"
]

instance DittoPlatform : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Open-source DT platform handling data exchange and service orchestration for real-time bidirectional communication in the LEED Platinum academic building's living lab"
]

instance UnrealEngine5Environment : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Rich visualization environment providing immersive 3D interaction with the DT for the LEED Platinum academic building's living lab"
]

instance EclipseHonoMiddleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Middleware platform enabling real-time bidirectional communication between physical devices and DT via Eclipse Hono protocol"
]

instance TwinLightControlFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "BIM-centric control framework implementing adaptive lighting algorithms (e.g., TwinLight) for automated control logic generation"
]

instance BIMToolsRevit : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "BIM modeling tools (e.g., Revit) used by Building Control Engineers to model devices and define control logic in the LEED Platinum academic building's living lab"
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring and visualization service providing immersive 3D interaction with the DT via Unreal Engine 5 for the LEED Platinum academic building's living lab"
    DTDFVocab:provides ImmersiveVisualizationInsight
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics and optimization service implementing reinforcement learning (BOREALIS) for energy efficiency, visual comfort, and operational cost reduction in the LEED Platinum academic building's living lab"
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyOptimizationInsight
]

instance AutomatedCommissioningService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated commissioning service enabling bidirectional communication between BIM and physical BCS via TwinLight's BIM-centric framework"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlLogicUpdateAction
]

instance CustomizablePluginService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Modular service extension framework allowing DT Engineers to develop custom plugins (e.g., IoT integration, AI analytics) for the LEED Platinum academic building's living lab"
    DTDFVocab:provides ExtensibilityAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance ImmersiveVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "3D interactive visualization of the DT enabling stakeholder collaboration across Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, and DT Engineers for the LEED Platinum academic building's living lab"
]

instance EnergyOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics results quantifying energy efficiency improvements through adaptive control strategies (e.g., reinforcement learning in BOREALIS) for the LEED Platinum academic building's living lab"
]

instance VisualComfortAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustment of lighting and HVAC parameters based on real-time occupancy and environmental data to optimize visual comfort in the LEED Platinum academic building's living lab"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ControlLogicUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated updates to BIM-based control strategies (e.g., adaptive lighting algorithms) triggered by live sensor feedback in the LEED Platinum academic building's living lab"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ODTEValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative metric (Overall Digital Twin Entanglement) validating DT interoperability and architectural coherence for the LEED Platinum academic building's living lab"
]

instance ExtensibilityAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic extension of DT functionality through custom plugins developed by DT Engineers without redeployment in the LEED Platinum academic building's living lab"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// Supporting Environment and Component relationships
instance LivingLabEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains BIMStaticModel, RuntimeOperationalData, TemperatureSensor, HumiditySensor, OccupancySensor, EnergyMonitor, CO2Sensor,
                 OccupancyActuator, HVACActuator, LightingActuator, RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveAnalyticsService,
                 AutomatedCommissioningService, CustomizablePluginService, UnrealEngine5Environment, EclipseHonoMiddleware
]

instance LEEDPlatinumBuilding : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "LEED Platinum certified academic building serving as the Living Lab case study for end-to-end DT deployment across design, construction, operation, and potential refurbishment phases"
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DT generates control signals and commands to physical actuators (e.g., lighting, HVAC systems) through middleware (Eclipse Hono/Ditto), enabling closed-loop operation. This interaction is facilitated by the framework’s automated generation of runtime interfaces from BIM-enriched models, ensuring bidirectional communication between the virtual and physical domains. Control logic is embedded directly into BIM models during device modeling, ensuring consistency and real-time adaptability."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The DT operates on a **real-time to near-real-time** time scale, with synchronization rates determined by sensor sampling frequencies and middleware protocols (Eclipse Hono/Ditto). For adaptive lighting (e.g., BOREALIS), iterative updates to control policies occur post-occupancy based on live sensor feedback, enabling dynamic optimization over time. The framework supports low-latency data exchange to ensure temporal coherence between physical and virtual representations."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is a **service-oriented, modular ecosystem** orchestrated through a centralized framework with the following components:
  1. **Core DT**: A unified virtual representation of the physical building, synchronized with real-time data via Hono/Ditto.
  2. **Middleware Layer**: Eclipse Hono/Ditto handles bidirectional communication between physical devices and the DT.
  3. **Visualization Layer**: Unreal Engine 5 provides immersive 3D interfaces for monitoring and control.
  4. **Service Layer**: Extensible extension points for DT Engineers to deploy custom services (e.g., analytics, IoT integration).
  5. **BIM Integration Layer**: Enriched BIM models serve as the semantic backbone for device modeling and runtime interface generation.
  The constellation ensures interoperability via standardized protocols and a unified architecture, enabling scalability across AECO projects."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT spans all lifecycle stages of the physical building:
  1. **Design Phase**: Enriched BIM models incorporate static and dynamic BCS components (e.g., sensors, actuators) for future-proofing.
  2. **Construction Phase**: BIM models are validated and transformed into runtime artifacts (e.g., middleware configurations) for automated deployment.
  3. **Operation Phase**: Real-time synchronization with physical sensors/actuators enables adaptive control (e.g., energy optimization via reinforcement learning in BOREALIS).
  4. **Refurbishment/Adaptation**: The modular architecture supports incremental updates to DT services and models without full redeployment. The framework ensures compatibility with existing AECO standards (e.g., BIM) throughout the lifecycle."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The DT’s fidelity and validity are ensured through:
  1. **ODTE Metric**: Evaluates the interoperability and architectural coherence of the DT by measuring its synchronization with the physical building.
  2. **Model Validation**: BIM models are validated before transformation to ensure semantic consistency and accuracy.
  3. **Runtime Synchronization**: Real-time data exchange via Hono/Ditto minimizes latency and drift between physical and virtual representations.
  4. **Uncertainty Handling**: The framework supports iterative updates (e.g., BOREALIS’ reinforcement learning) to adapt control policies to evolving operational conditions.
  5. **Verification/Validation**: The Living Lab case study demonstrates the DT’s effectiveness in optimizing energy efficiency and operational costs, validating its practical applicability."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT technical connection employs **Eclipse Hono and Ditto middleware** for real-time data exchange, facilitating bidirectional communication between physical sensors and the DT. The architecture supports **MQTT/WebSocket protocols** (as implied by DT frameworks like Ditto) for low-latency, scalable connectivity, ensuring seamless integration with Building Control Systems."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is deployed as a **modular, service-oriented platform** with standardized extension points, enabling scalable hosting on centralized or distributed infrastructures. The framework automates the deployment process from BIM models to runtime artifacts, reducing manual effort while supporting edge-to-cloud integration for operational flexibility."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The DT integrates horizontally with:
  1. **External IoT Systems**: Custom services (DT Engineers) extend functionality to include third-party IoT gateways and analytics platforms.
  2. **BIM Collaboration Tools**: Enriched BIM models serve as a semantic backbone for interoperability with design/construction tools (e.g., Revit).
  3. **Building Management Systems (BMS)**: Middleware (Hono/Ditto) bridges the DT with legacy BMS for bidirectional control.
  4. **Stakeholder Workflows**: Unified interfaces (Unreal Engine 5) enable collaboration across roles (e.g., Facility Managers, DT Engineers) without siloed tools."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework adheres to the following standards and methodologies:
  1. **BIM Standards**: AECO-specific standards (e.g., IFC, COBie) for modeling building components and their relationships.
  2. **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE)**: Standardized transformations (model-to-model/model-to-text) for automating DT deployment.
  3. **Middleware Standards**: Eclipse Hono/Ditto follow open-source protocols (e.g., MQTT, WebSockets) for interoperability.
  4. **Unreal Engine 5**: Industry-standard 3D visualization platform for DT interaction.
  5. **AECO Workflows**: Aligns with existing BIM-based engineering practices (e.g., TwinLight/BOREALIS projects)."
]
